events.text: Patient is a 74-year-old female of Asian race with an ECOG performance status of 2
At age 74, patient presented with progressive cervical lymphadenopathy, fatigue, and unintentional weight loss of 12 pounds, with incidental finding of anemia on routine labs
At age 74, excisional biopsy of a left cervical lymph node revealed diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, ICD-O site C77.9, histology code 9680/3

At age 74, pathology report showed diffuse effacement by large atypical lymphoid cells, Ki-67 of 65 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, BCL6, MUM1, negative for CD10, BCL2 positive by IHC, consistent with non-GCB (ABC) subtype by Hans algorithm
At age 74, flow cytometry demonstrated monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, BCL6, MUM1, negative for CD10, CD5, CD23
At age 74, FISH analysis showed no MYC rearrangement, no BCL2 rearrangement, BCL6 rearrangement positive; single-hit BCL6, not classified as double-hit lymphoma
At age 74, laboratory studies showed LDH elevated at 412 U/L, CBC with WBC 5.4, hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL, platelets 134, CMP with albumin 3.0 g/dL, total protein 8.4 g/dL

At age 74, staging PET/CT showed FDG-avid bilateral cervical, bilateral axillary, and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, largest cervical node 4.3 cm with SUVmax 16.7, FDG-avid splenic lesions suggesting splenic involvement
At age 74, bone marrow biopsy showed 10 percent involvement by large B-cell lymphoma with paratrabecular pattern
At age 74, patient was staged as Ann Arbor Stage IV with B symptoms (weight loss), IPI score 4 (high risk) based on age over 60, elevated LDH, Stage IV, and ECOG 2

At age 74, after geriatric oncology assessment, patient initiated cycle 1 of R-miniCHOP (dose-attenuated R-CHOP per LYSA protocol for elderly): rituximab 375 mg/m2, cyclophosphamide 400 mg/m2, doxorubicin 25 mg/m2, vincristine 1 mg flat dose, prednisone 40 mg/m2
At age 74, after cycle 1, patient developed grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 nausea, with grade 2 neutropenia managed with pegfilgrastim
At age 74, patient received cycle 2 of R-miniCHOP, tolerated reasonably well
At age 74, after cycle 2, patient was hospitalized for grade 3 neutropenic fever with ANC 320 and temperature 102.8F, treated with ceftriaxone and discharged after 4 days
At age 74, patient received cycle 3 of R-miniCHOP with prophylactic levofloxacin added

At age 74, interim PET/CT after 3 cycles showed partial response with cervical lymphadenopathy decreased to 2.1 cm, SUVmax 6.2, Deauville score 4, splenic lesions resolved, persistent mild retroperitoneal uptake
At age 74, patient received cycles 4, 5, and 6 of R-miniCHOP, completing planned treatment
At age 74, after cycle 4, patient developed grade 2 peripheral neuropathy with difficulty walking, vincristine discontinued for cycles 5 and 6
At age 74, after cycle 5, patient developed herpes zoster on right T4 dermatome, treated with valacyclovir and continued on prophylaxis
At age 74, end-of-treatment PET/CT showed near-complete metabolic response with small residual cervical node 1.2 cm, SUVmax 3.4, Deauville score 3, interpreted as likely complete response
At age 74, patient was deemed to have achieved complete remission and was placed on surveillance, with acyclovir prophylaxis continued
At age 75, at 12-month follow-up, patient reports persistent grade 2 neuropathy limiting ambulation, using a cane, otherwise no evidence of recurrence on clinical exam and labs

PATHOLOGY REPORT

DIAGNOSIS AND FINDINGS:
At age 74, pathology report showed diffuse effacement by large atypical lymphoid cells, Ki-67 of 65 percent, immunohistochemistry positive for CD20, CD19, BCL6, MUM1, negative for CD10, BCL2 positive by IHC, consistent with non-GCB (ABC) subtype by Hans algorithm

CLINICAL PROGRESS NOTE

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
At age 74, flow cytometry demonstrated monoclonal B-cell population positive for CD20, CD19, BCL6, MUM1, negative for CD10, CD5, CD23

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

Please correlate clinically.

The patient was seen in clinic today for ongoing oncologic management.

scenario_blurb: Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center and activated B-cell subtypes. Ann Arbor stage II-IV. Treated with R-CHOP. Include IPI scoring, PET-CT response assessment, some with relapse requiring R-ICE salvage and autologous stem cell transplant.
